crime.value: Murder of Thomas Jacks, town founder, shot and killed
crime.quote: Tom's murderer would be hung, if not directly, indirectly for the killing

victim.value: Thomas Jacks, town founder who was making advances on the narrator's wife Ellie
victim.quote: I saw my good friend, Thomas Jacks riding away from my house... This gave me a bad feeling

closed_world.value: Cherry Creek, a small isolated frontier town with about 30 families
closed_world.quote: Cherry Creek wasn't the type of town where one would expect trouble. It was a small out of the way place

culprit_and_motive.value: Jacob Star (the narrator) killed Thomas Jacks because he believed Tom was having an affair with his wife
culprit_and_motive.quote: I was content that Ellie never had to feel Tom's eyes on her again

resolution.value: The real killer goes unpunished as another man (Yates) will hang for a different crime, and Jacob is offered the sheriff position
resolution.quote: they wanted me to become the town sheriff, she smiled

investigator.value: Jacob Star, the actual murderer telling his own story in first person
investigator.quote: I too, was here for the peace... Now I played a role in the serenity of our little settlement

suspect_architecture.value: Single suspect architecture with false external culprit - narrator Jacob Star is the actual murderer while Yates becomes convenient scapegoat
suspect_architecture.quote: Simply put; our case was solved and the guilty man would hang. There would be no need for a trial.

red_herring.value: The star drawn in sand by dying Thomas Jacks, interpreted as identifying external killer Yates rather than narrator Jacob Star
red_herring.quote: they would put it together when they saw the star in the sand which Tom had drawn

clue_fairness.value: Star symbol clue presented fairly - reader sees both the physical evidence and learns narrator's surname is Star before the reveal
clue_fairness.quote: Sheriff Jacob Star. I like the sound of that.

social_world.value: Small frontier town hierarchy centered on Thomas Jacks as founding father and community leader, with narrator as respected law enforcement
social_world.quote: The residents regarded him as sort of the 'father of the town'. It seemed everything he did was for the town

alibi.value: Narrator's early return from Lexington business trip - meant to surprise wife but instead discovers Thomas Jacks leaving his house
alibi.quote: I had been gone to Lexington to work on a deal to sell the army some beef

reveal_mechanic.value: Dramatic irony revelation where narrator's internal thoughts expose him as the killer while community mourns victim as hero
reveal_mechanic.quote: My real thoughts were how justice had been served all the way around. Tom deserved to die

media_and_audience.value: Community narrative transforms Thomas Jacks into martyred founding father legend while true killer becomes celebrated sheriff
media_and_audience.quote: Someday there might even be talk of a statue of old Tom put on the town square and I would vote in favor

investigator_wound.value: Suspected marital infidelity and betrayal by trusted community leader - Civil War trauma mentioned but adultery concern drives the crime
investigator_wound.quote: This gave me a bad feeling as to the reason why a man was leaving my house and my wife in a suspicious manner

_meta.title: ebook jay wilson the murder of cherry creek
